title: Write the Right .gitignore

problem.statement: You're setting up a repo containing a Python virtual environment folder venv/, a .env file with secrets, Terraform state files (*.tfstate), and compiled __pycache__/ folders. Write the contents of a .gitignore file that excludes all of these from version control.
problem.use_case: Committing secrets (.env), machine-specific virtualenvs, and generated state/bytecode files is one of the most common real-world Git mistakes — a correct .gitignore in the very first commit prevents all of them.

hints: Trailing slash (venv/) means 'this directory, wherever it appears' — good practice for folder excludes.
Don't forget .env — committing secrets is one of the most common real-world Git mistakes.
Terraform state files contain sensitive infra data and should never be committed either.

solution.explanation: Each pattern excludes a category of file that's either environment-specific, secret, or generated/compiled — none belong in version control: venv/, .env, *.tfstate, __pycache__/ (and typically *.pyc, .terraform/ alongside them).
